diagrams.title: Disk Geometry - Track, Sector, Cylinder
diagrams.description: Shows the physical organization of data on a magnetic disk with tracks, sectors, and cylinders
elements: Cylinder (vertical stack of same-radius tracks across platters)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how data is physically organized and accessed on magnetic disks

diagrams.title: Disk Scheduling Algorithms Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, and C-SCAN algorithms showing head movement patterns
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how different algorithms order disk requests and their head movement patterns

diagrams.title: Disk Access Time Components
diagrams.description: Breakdown of disk access time showing seek time, rotational delay, and transfer time
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the mathematical components of disk I/O performance

diagrams.title: Disk Partition and Boot Sector Structure
diagrams.description: Shows MBR location, partition table entries, and boot sector structure
diagrams.purpose: Understanding disk organization for booting and partition management
